problem_statement: Flight search availability filtering is inverted: GET /flights/search with only_available=true returns fully booked flights, and only_available=false hides flights that still have seats available.
goal: Fix search_flights so the only_available flag excludes flights with no remaining seats when true, and includes all flights when false (or otherwise applies the intended availability filter correctly).
acceptance_criteria: With only_available=true, search results exclude any flight where all seats are already booked.
With only_available=false, flights with remaining seats are not incorrectly hidden.
Behavior is consistent with the default request path for GET /flights/search.
constraints: Keep changes localized to the flight search availability filter logic if possible.
Preserve existing search behavior unrelated to availability filtering.
unknowns: Exact implementation of seat availability calculation in search_flights.
Whether tests already exist for the availability filter behavior and need updating.
repo_summary: Repository includes backend API, service, model, schema, and tests for flight search; the reported issue points directly to backend/app/services/flight_service.py search_flights.

targeted_symbols.reason: Reported affected file and method; likely contains the inverted only_available filter logic.
Likely place to validate the corrected availability behavior and prevent regression.

localization_rationale: The issue description explicitly names backend/app/services/flight_service.py::search_flights as the affected file.
The bug is about a boolean filter being inverted, which is most likely implemented in the service-layer query construction rather than in the route or schema.
Tests for service behavior are the smallest complementary area for verifying the fix.
